data.title: POSCO Posts Higher Q1 2026 Revenue but Sharply Lower Operating Profit
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from POSCO ( (PKX) ) is now available.

POSCO, the main steelmaking subsidiary of POSCO HOLDINGS, reported provisional separate financial results for the first quarter of 2026, compiled under K-IFRS and subject to review. Revenue for Q1 2026 came in at KRW 8.94 trillion, up 7.7% from the fourth quarter of 2025 but slightly down 0.4% from the first quarter of 2025.

Operating profit declined to KRW 0.21 trillion, falling 36.6% quarter-on-quarter and 38.4% year-on-year, signaling margin pressure despite stable top-line performance. Profit before income tax rose 10.6% versus Q4 2025 to KRW 0.24 trillion but was still 14.3% lower than a year earlier, while bottom-line profit dropped about 21% sequentially and 18% year-on-year, indicating a tougher earnings environment for the steel business in early 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (PKX) stock is a Sell with a $36.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on POSCO stock, see the PKX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PKX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PKX is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weakening fundamentals and cash conversion (margin compression and negative free cash flow), with additional pressure from a high P/E. Technicals are supportive due to strong trend strength, but overbought signals temper that benefit. Earnings call signals potential 2026 catalysts (lithium ramp and restructuring) but with meaningful execution and macro risks.

To see Spark’s full report on PKX stock, click here.

More about POSCO

POSCO HOLDINGS INC. is a South Korea-based global steelmaker, with its core subsidiary POSCO engaged in producing and selling steel products for automotive, construction, shipbuilding and other industrial sectors. The group reports under Korean International Financial Reporting Standards and is listed in the U.S. as a foreign private issuer, filing annual reports on Form 20-F.

Average Trading Volume: 187,141

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $23.33B

data.title: ArcelorMittal Posts Strong Q1 2026 and Positions for European Market Upswing
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ArcelorMittal ( (MT) ) has provided an update.

On April 30, 2026, ArcelorMittal reported resilient first‑quarter 2026 results, with EBITDA per tonne rising to $131, net income of $0.6 billion and record iron ore production and shipments in Liberia. The company highlighted a sharp improvement in safety performance, a seasonal working‑capital driven free‑cash outflow that lifted net debt to $9.3 billion but left liquidity at $9.9 billion, and ongoing returns to shareholders including a first quarterly dividend and continued buybacks reducing the share count by 38% since September 2020.

Management stressed that new European measures such as CBAM and a tariff rate quota effective from July 1, 2026 are expected to structurally improve the region’s steel market, supporting higher utilization, pricing and profitability that ArcelorMittal aims to capture by restarting idled blast furnaces and expanding electric‑arc furnace capacity. The group is maintaining 2026 capex guidance of $4.5‑$5.0 billion, with $1.7‑$2.0 billion earmarked for high‑return strategic projects that are projected to add about $1.8 billion of incremental EBITDA, underpinning its positive free‑cash‑flow outlook and balanced capital allocation between growth investment and shareholder returns.

The most recent analyst rating on (MT) stock is a Hold with a $60.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ArcelorMittal stock, see the MT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MT is a Neutral.

MT scores mid-range primarily due to solid financial resilience (low leverage) and improved 2025 profitability, tempered by weak recent free-cash-flow conversion. Technically, the uptrend is strong, but overbought indicators increase near-term risk. Valuation is only modestly supportive (mid P/E, low yield), while the latest earnings call adds a positive outlook and project-driven growth narrative with identifiable cost, policy, and legal uncertainties.

To see Spark’s full report on MT stock, click here.

ArcelorMittal is the world’s leading integrated steel and mining company, with shares listed in New York, Amsterdam, Paris, Luxembourg and Madrid. The Group produces crude steel and iron ore for global markets and operates a diversified asset portfolio across Europe, the Americas, Africa and Asia, serving automotive, construction, energy and industrial customers worldwide.

data.title: Global Water Reaches Arizona Settlement on Key Rate Cases
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Global Water Resources ( (GWRS) ) is now available.

On April 28 and 29, 2026, Global Water Resources and Arizona regulators reached and disclosed a unanimous settlement covering rate cases for its GW-Santa Cruz and GW-Palo Verde utilities, following original filings made on March 5, 2025. The deal proposes a $2.3 million annual revenue increase for GW-Santa Cruz, based on a $63.6 million rate base and 9.6% return on equity, with new rates targeted for November 1, 2026 and an estimated $2.68 monthly bill increase for a median residential water user.

As part of the settlement, the GW-Palo Verde rate case would be withdrawn and refiled in 2027 using a 2026 test year, while GW-Palo Verde seeks to boost a temporary bill credit to customers by about $0.4 million annually until that case is resolved. The arrangement, which remains subject to Arizona Corporation Commission approval, is positioned by management as a key step toward incorporating major water and wastewater assets into base rates, supporting infrastructure investment while moderating customer impacts and setting the stage for a pivotal 2027 rate filing.

The most recent analyst rating on (GWRS) stock is a Buy with a $12.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Global Water Resources stock, see the GWRS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GWRS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GWRS is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by mixed financial performance (improving balance sheet and revenue growth but sharply weaker margins and deeply negative free cash flow). Technicals also weigh on the rating given the clear downtrend despite oversold signals. Valuation is a headwind due to a very high P/E, while earnings-call commentary supports longer-term growth but underscores near-term earnings, expense, and regulatory timing risks.

To see Spark’s full report on GWRS stock, click here.

More about Global Water Resources

Global Water Resources, Inc. is a NASDAQ-listed water resource management company that owns and operates 39 systems providing water, wastewater and recycled water services, primarily in high-growth corridors around metropolitan Phoenix and Tucson. The company is recognized for its Total Water Management approach and extensive water reuse programs, recycling more than 1 billion gallons annually in water-scarce, fast-growing areas.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The latest announcement is out from Amtech Systems ( (ASYS) ).

On April 24, 2026, Amtech Systems’ board approved the appointment of veteran finance executive Thomas Sabol as Chief Financial Officer, effective May 14, 2026, succeeding interim CFO Mark Weaver and also making Sabol the company’s principal accounting and financial officer. The move brings Amtech a leader with more than three decades of senior financial experience, including IPO execution, SEC reporting, and international expansion at several technology-focused firms, and is structured with a mix of salary, bonus, and equity incentives alongside change-in-control and severance protections that underscore the strategic importance of the role.

Under an offer letter dated April 17, 2026, Sabol’s package includes a $350,000 base salary, eligibility for an annual bonus targeted at 50% of salary split between cash and restricted stock, and a grant of 45,000 stock options vesting over three years, subject to board approval. The compensation design, coupled with at-will terms and defined benefits in the event of a change in control or termination without cause, aligns Sabol’s interests with long-term shareholder value while signaling the company’s emphasis on stable, experienced financial stewardship after a period under an interim CFO.

The most recent analyst rating on (ASYS) stock is a Buy with a $16.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Amtech Systems stock, see the ASYS Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Stellantis Returns to Profit in Q1 2026 and Confirms Full-Year Guidance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Stellantis ( (IT:STLAM) ).

Stellantis N.V. reported first-quarter 2026 results on April 30, showing a return to profitability as net revenues rose 6% year over year to €38.1 billion, driven mainly by higher volumes in North America and gains in Europe and the Middle East & Africa. Net profit swung to €0.4 billion from a loss a year earlier, while adjusted operating income climbed to €1.0 billion with a 2.5% margin and most regions in positive territory.

Industrial free cash flow remained negative at €1.9 billion but improved 37% versus Q1 2025 despite significant cash outflows linked to second-half 2025 charges, and liquidity stayed robust at €44.1 billion, within the company’s targeted range. Stellantis further bolstered its balance sheet in March 2026 by issuing €5 billion in hybrid perpetual notes and reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance for higher net revenues, better margins and improved industrial free cash flow, underscoring management’s confidence in a gradual, sustainable recovery trajectory.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:STLAM) stock is a Buy with a EUR9.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Stellantis stock, see the IT:STLAM Stock Forecast page.

More about Stellantis

Stellantis N.V. is a global automotive manufacturer, producing passenger cars, commercial vehicles and related mobility solutions under multiple brands, with significant operations in North America, Enlarged Europe and the Middle East & Africa. The company focuses on driving profitable growth through new vehicle launches, margin improvement and disciplined capital and liquidity management across its industrial operations.

data.title: BBVA Refreshes Key Board Committees to Strengthen Oversight
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Banco Bilbao ( (BBVA) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 29, 2026, BBVA’s Board of Directors approved changes to the composition of several key board committees, reshaping the Audit, Remuneration, and Risk and Compliance Committees while leaving other strategic committees unchanged. The reshuffle, disclosed from Madrid on April 30, 2026, adjusts the mix of independent directors in core oversight roles, signaling a renewed emphasis on audit quality, pay governance, and risk and compliance oversight without altering the bank’s existing executive or technology-focused governance structure.

The Audit Committee will now be chaired by José Miguel Andrés Torrecillas and include members Enrique Casanueva Nárdiz, Sonia Dulá, Connie Hedegaard Koksbang, and Jorge Montalbo Todolí, while the Remuneration Committee will be led by Ana Revenga Shanklin alongside four other members, and the Risk and Compliance Committee will be chaired by Raúl Galamba de Oliveira with a refreshed membership. These changes, which leave the Executive, Appointments and Corporate Governance, and Technology and Cybersecurity Committees intact, aim to fine-tune BBVA’s board architecture at a time when global banks face heightened scrutiny over financial reporting, compensation structures, and regulatory risk management.

The most recent analyst rating on (BBVA) stock is a Buy with a $23.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Banco Bilbao stock, see the BBVA Stock Forecast page.

Ametek ( (AME) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 30, 2026, industrial technology group AMETEK reported record first-quarter 2026 results, with sales up 11% year on year to $1.93 billion and adjusted earnings per share rising 13% to $1.97. The company highlighted strong organic growth, contributions from recent acquisitions, and core margin expansion, as well as a 23% jump in orders that pushed backlog to a record level.

The Electronic Instruments Group posted 11% sales growth and margin improvement, while the Electromechanical Group delivered record sales of $663.9 million and a 33% increase in operating income, underscoring broad-based strength across its portfolio. Reflecting this momentum, AMETEK raised its full-year 2026 guidance, now expecting high single-digit sales growth versus 2025 and higher adjusted EPS, signaling confidence in continued demand and its capacity to deploy capital into further strategic acquisitions.

AMETEK also disclosed on April 30, 2026, that it had entered into a definitive agreement to acquire First Aviation Services, Inc., aligning with its stated focus on strategic acquisitions. The transaction is expected to enhance its position in attractive aerospace and aviation-related niches and further leverage the company’s growth model and balance sheet strength for long-term value creation for stakeholders.

At FB Bancorp, Inc.’s Annual Meeting of Stockholders held on April 29, 2026, shareholders elected three directors, J. Luis Baños, Jr., Gerard W. Barousse, Jr., and Mark C. data.title: OSR Holdings Grants BCME Exclusive Global VXM01 Rights
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

OSR Holdings ( (OSRH) ) has provided an update.

On April 29, 2026, OSR Holdings signed a global exclusive license agreement with largest shareholder BCM Europe AG, granting BCME worldwide rights to develop and commercialize VXM01, a Phase 3‑ready oral VEGFR‑2‑targeting cancer immunotherapy, in return for up to $815 million in milestone payments, downstream royalties, and an option requiring BCME to purchase up to $15 million of OSR stock. In a related April 29, 2026 transaction, BCME and affiliates agreed to pledge their entire approximately 29.7% OSR stake—9,974,728 shares—as collateral securing milestone obligations, while OSR moves to acquire full VXM01 intellectual property from subsidiary Vaximm for $30 million, a structure that tightens alignment between the company and its largest shareholder and underscores confidence in VXM01’s commercial potential.

The agreement is structured so BCME funds all development, regulatory, and commercialization work, including any sublicenses, with OSR to receive 100% of future royalties after BCME recoups its investment and preferred return. Governance, IP, and termination terms are described as customary, and the related‑party deal was approved by OSR’s board, including independent directors, after an external fairness opinion, positioning OSR to capture long‑term value from VXM01 while limiting its direct development spend.

More about OSR Holdings

OSR Holdings, Inc. is a global healthcare holding company focused on advancing biomedical innovations in health and wellness through subsidiaries active in immuno-oncology, regenerative biologics, and medical device technologies. The company, listed on Nasdaq under the ticker OSRH, seeks to improve health outcomes worldwide by developing and commercializing novel therapeutic platforms and related technologies.

data.description:

The latest update is out from Timberland Bancorp ( (TSBK) ).

Timberland Bancorp reported solid results for its second fiscal quarter ended March 31, 2026, with net income rising 6% year over year to $7.13 million, or $0.90 per diluted share, though earnings fell 13% from the prior quarter on higher credit loss provisions and slightly lower net interest income. Key profitability metrics remained strong, including a 1.43% return on average assets, a 10.72% return on average equity and a 3.81% net interest margin, while the bank’s balance sheet grew, deposits increased 6% year over year, and the board declared a $0.29 quarterly dividend, extending a 54-quarter payout streak despite a rise in non-performing assets tied largely to a single participation loan.

For the first half of fiscal 2026, net income climbed 13% to $15.35 million, or $1.94 per diluted share, supported by an 8% increase in operating revenue and a 9% gain in net interest income versus the same period in 2025. Management highlighted stable margin performance amid Federal Reserve rate cuts, strong liquidity with minimal borrowings and substantial unused funding capacity, continued share repurchases and encouraging early traction at the new University Place branch, underscoring confidence in Timberland’s capital position and long-term operating strategy.

Spark’s Take on TSBK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSBK is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by healthy financial fundamentals (strong margins, conservative leverage, and positive free cash flow) and supportive technicals (price above key moving averages with positive MACD). Emera ( (TSE:EMA) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 30, 2026, Emera announced it has issued a notice of redemption for all US$1.2 billion of its remaining outstanding 6.75% Fixed-to-Floating Subordinated Notes, Series 2016-A, which are due 2076. The company will redeem the notes at 100% of principal plus accrued and unpaid interest, with settlement to occur through the Depository Trust Company on June 15, 2026, effectively retiring this tranche of higher-coupon hybrid debt and adjusting its capital structure.

The redemption, scheduled for June 15, 2026, removes a sizable long-dated subordinated obligation from Emera’s balance sheet. This move may reduce future interest expense on this legacy security and could be seen as a sign of balance sheet management and funding flexibility that is relevant for bondholders and other capital-market stakeholders.

Empro Group Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors that it will be late in submitting its Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. The delayed filing concerns the company’s annual disclosure to U.S. markets, including audited financial statements and key operational updates.

The company cites time pressure around compiling, disseminating and reviewing the information required for the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) as the main reason for missing the deadline. Management states that meeting the original due date would have imposed undue hardship and expense, but it does not point to auditor changes, control failures or special committee reviews.

Empro Group Inc. has committed to filing the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) within fifteen calendar days after the original due date, as permitted under SEC Rule 12b-25. Investors should therefore expect the annual report to appear in early May 2026, assuming there are no further complications.

The company affirms that all other required SEC periodic reports over the last 12 months have been filed on time, which helps support a picture of general compliance. Importantly, Empro does not anticipate any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior year, and it has not provided preliminary earnings figures or other forward-looking financial projections in this notice.

Empro emphasizes that it is working to complete the remaining annual-report work while staying within regulatory requirements. data.title: Sinovac Biotech Delays Key Annual 20-F Filing
data.description:
Sinovac Biotech ( (SVA) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sinovac Biotech Ltd. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors that it will not meet the deadline for its Annual Report on Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the year ended December 31, 2025. The delay relates directly to a major legal and auditing disruption that has forced the company to revisit several prior years of financial reporting.

In January 2025, the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council of the United Kingdom issued a ruling concerning Sinovac, triggering significant knock-on effects. By April 15, 2025, its then-auditor Grant Thornton Zhitong Certified Public Accountants LLP resigned and simultaneously advised that its audit opinions for 2021, 2022, and 2023 should no longer be relied upon.

Following this, Sinovac appointed Zhonghua Certified Public Accountants LLP on December 31, 2025 to take over as independent auditor. Zhonghua has been engaged to perform integrated audits for 2025 and 2024 and financial statement audits for 2023, 2022, and 2021, reflecting the depth of the restatement work now required.

The company has concluded that both its common and preferred shares need retrospective adjustment in light of the Privy Council ruling. As a result, its consolidated financial statements for 2021 through 2023 must be restated, which is the central reason for the late filing of the 2025 Form 20-F (Yearly Report).

Sinovac has indicated, under the relief provided by Form 12b-25, that it expects to submit the delayed annual report within the 15-day grace period following the original deadline. Management states that the 2025 Annual Report will be filed as soon as practicable once the new auditor completes the necessary audit and restatement procedures.

The company acknowledged that it has already experienced prior delays, including the 2024 Form 20-F (Yearly Report), which was only filed on April 30, 2026 due to the same underlying issues. However, Sinovac does not currently anticipate any significant change in its results of operations versus the prior year based on information expected to appear in the upcoming 2025 filing.

Investors should note that any expectations regarding timing, restatement impacts, and audit completion are inherently forward-looking and subject to change. While the company has not provided preliminary financial figures, the extensive audit and restatement process could affect investor sentiment until the updated annual reports are available.

Sinovac emphasizes that it is working to complete the integrated audits and restatements to return to a normal reporting schedule. The notification was signed on April 30, 2026 by Chief Financial Officer Nan Wang, signaling executive-level oversight of the compliance effort and the company’s intention to resolve its reporting delays.

Spark’s Take on SVA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SVA is a Neutral.

Sinovac Biotech’s overall score reflects its strong liquidity and balance sheet, offset by poor financial performance and lack of earnings. The technical indicators suggest neutrality, and the valuation appears unattractive due to the negative P/E ratio.

To see Spark’s full report on SVA stock, click here.

More about Sinovac Biotech

Sinovac Biotech Ltd. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on the research, development, manufacturing, and commercialization of vaccines. Its products target infectious diseases, and the company is known globally for its role in vaccine supply from its base in Beijing’s Haidian District.

data.title: Fitness Champs Holdings to Implement 30-for-1 Share Consolidation to Maintain Nasdaq Compliance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Fitness Champs Holdings Limited ( (FCHL) ) has issued an announcement.

Fitness Champs Holdings Limited, a leading aquatic sports education provider in Singapore, offers swimming and lifesaving lessons to children and adults and is a major SwimSafer provider for public school students. The company has built its brand “Fitness Champs” since 2012 and seeks to broaden its reach by moving into other sports education segments over time.

On March 24, 2026, Fitness Champs’ board approved a 30-for-1 consolidation of its authorized, issued and outstanding shares, which will become effective in the market on May 4, 2026. The move, announced on April 30, 2026, will reduce the Class A share count from about 36.95 million to roughly 1.23 million and Class B shares from about 580,524 to around 19,351, helping the company maintain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price rules and potentially stabilizing its trading profile for investors.

From the start of trading on May 4, 2026, Fitness Champs’ Class A ordinary shares will trade on a split-adjusted basis on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the existing symbol FCHL but with a new CUSIP number. Shareholders will not need to take any action for the consolidation, as every 30 ordinary shares will automatically convert into one, with no fractional shares issued and any fractional entitlements rounded up to one whole share per holder.

Spark’s Take on FCHL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FCHL is a Underperform.

The score is primarily pressured by weak financial performance quality (margin deterioration, weakened cash generation, and a sharply higher leverage profile with very thin equity). Technicals add further caution with negative momentum and a broader downtrend. Valuation offers limited support due to a negative P/E and no dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on FCHL stock, click here.

More about Fitness Champs Holdings Limited

Fitness Champs Holdings Limited is a Singapore-based aquatic sports education provider offering general swimming lessons for children and adults, including ladies-only classes, as well as competitive swimming and lifesaving courses. The company is one of the largest providers of swimming lessons to children in Singapore’s public schools through the Ministry of Education’s SwimSafer program and has operated private lessons under the “Fitness Champs” brand since 2012, with plans to expand into other sports such as pickleball.

data.title: Golden Entertainment Announces Delisting and Leadership Transition
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Golden Entertainment ( (GDEN) ) just unveiled an update.

Golden Entertainment has fully repaid and terminated its first-lien credit facility with JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A., resulting in the discharge of related obligations and the release of associated liens, simplifying the company’s balance sheet. In connection with a broader set of completed transactions, the company accelerated, vested and settled all outstanding stock options, RSUs, PSUs and related restricted stock as of the equity award settlement date, paying out in shares or cash net of tax withholdings and treating incomplete performance periods at least at target.

On April 30, 2026, Golden Entertainment’s registrant entity notified Nasdaq that these transactions had closed and requested suspension and delisting of New HoldCo common stock, as well as deregistration steps that will end its periodic reporting duties, effectively taking the company’s listed equity off the public markets. Following closing, all pre-transaction directors resigned, senior officers left their roles, and the employment of longtime leader Blake L. Sartini was terminated with severance under his contract, marking a wholesale governance and leadership transition alongside the change in ownership and listing status.

The most recent analyst rating on (GDEN) stock is a Hold with a $28.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Golden Entertainment stock, see the GDEN Stock Forecast page.
